name: CRANIOVERTEBRAL JUNCTION (CVJ
chapter: DIFFERENTIAL DIAGNOSIS OF SKULL AND SPINE DISORDERS
dominant_finding: tip of odontoid process may lie up to 1 ± 6.6 mm above the Chamberlain line
distribution: usually falls tangent to posterior aspect of tip of odontoid process
discriminating_features: tip of odontoid process usually lies below / tangent to Chamberlain line by > 3 mm
tip of odontoid process may lie up to 1 ± 6.6 mm above the Chamberlain line
tip of odontoid < 4.5-5.0 mm above this line
usually falls tangent to posterior aspect of tip of odontoid process
ranges from 150° in flexion to 180° in extension
